name: Qena Governorate (Egypt)

colors.symbolism: the desert sands and wheat of Upper Egypt, central to Qena's agricultural identity
the cultivated land along the Nile in the Qena bend
details of the emblem and the ancient temples of Dendera and the Theban region

description: A yellow field charged in the centre with the green-and-brown emblem of Qena Governorate.
history: The flag of Qena Governorate displays its emblem on a yellow field, adopted as part of the system of governorate flags introduced in the 2000s. Qena lies in Upper Egypt at the great bend of the Nile and includes the Temple of Dendera and Luxor's hinterland.

features.symbolism: the desert and the sun of Upper Egypt and the wheat of the Qena bend of the Nile
the emblem of Qena with motifs of the Nile, agriculture and ancient Egyptian heritage of Upper Egypt
